name: Southern Fried Tofu
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Sesame oil, canola oil or other oil for frying
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain and rinse the tofu. Slice it horizontally into two even pieces. Then cut each of these pieces into 8 cubes.
2. Place all the cubes in a bowl and add 3 tablespoons soup base. Gently mix well and set aside.
3. Stir together half a cup of unbleached flour and the remaining 3 tablespoons soup base. Set aside this breading mixture.
4. Mix three-quarters of a cup of unbleached flour, gluten flour, salt, and spice blend. Blend well so the gluten flour does not create lumps. Stir in 1 tablespoon water and 1 tablespoon canola oil. If the gluten flour creates lumps, blend the batter in a blender.
5. Heat the oil in a deep skillet. Dip a piece of tofu into the breading mixture and coat it completely. Then dip it into the batter, using prongs. Remove and hold it above the batter for about 10 seconds, tapping gently against the side of the bowl to release excess batter. Hold one-third of the piece of tofu in the hot oil for about 15 seconds to sear the bottom so it won't stick to the pan. Release the tofu and let it completely submerge.
6. Deep-fry the tofu until light brown. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels for 30 seconds. Place the tofu in the oven at 250 degrees for up to 15 minutes to retain crispness.
7. To serve, pour equal portions of Southern Country Gravy into each plate and set the hot fried tofu into the gravy.
